center.name: 欣古河
center.summary: 欣古河是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联18种鸟类，包括塔帕諾斯隱蜂鳥、Pearly parakeet、Snethlage's tody-tyrant等18种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Alta Floresta antpitta is found in the Amazon Basin of south-central Brazil, south of the Amazon between the Madeira and Xingu rivers

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Brazil from the upper Purus River northeast to Pará where it occurs between the Tapajós and Xingu rivers, and south and west into northern Mato Grosso state, southeastern Peru, and north-central Bolivia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The other population also occurs south of the Amazon, between the Xingu River and the lower Tocantins River. The black-bellied gnateater inhabits thick vegetation in terra firme forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: A second population is found from extreme northeastern Bolivia across Amazonian Brazil south of the Amazon River into the watersheds of the Madeira, Tapajós, Xingu, and Tocantins rivers. A third, much smaller, population is found in the area of Iguaçu Falls along the Argentina-Brazil border. That population possibly extends into Paraguay, where the South American Classification Committee has unconfirmed records. There are also scattered records at several locations from southeastern to northeastern Brazil. The black-collared swallow inhabits riverine belts, especially blackwater rivers, where it favors areas near waterfalls and along rapids. It elevation it reaches only about above sea level.{{cite book | last1 =McMullan | first1 =Miles | last2 =Donegan | first2 =Thomas M

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: interposita is found in the Amazon Basin south of the Amazon between the Xingu and Tocantins rivers in eastern Pará state

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: coerulescens is found in eastern Pará between the Xingu and Tocantins rivers

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: trumaii, the upper Rio Xingu in Brazil's Mato Grosso state The plain-brown woodcreeper inhabits a variety of humid forest landscapes

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: hellmayri: Brazil east of the Xingu River The plain-throated antwren primarily inhabits terra firme evergreen forest, and also várzea, igapó, and transitional forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Sclater's antwren is found in the upper Amazon Basin south of the Amazon River, in eastern Peru, northern Bolivia, and western Brazil east to the Xingu River and south to Mato Grosso state

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Snethlage's antpitta is found in northeastern Brazil south of the Amazon from the Xingu River east to the Atlantic

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Brazil's Pará state in the area of the lower Xingu and Tocantins rivers

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: carajaensis, Amazonian Brazil south of the Amazon between the Zingu and Tocantins/Araguaia rivers The strong-billed woodcreeper inhabits a very wide variety of temperate, subtropical, and tropical forested landscapes

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Tapajos fire-eye is found in central Amazonian Brazil south of the Amazon between the Tapajos and Xingu rivers

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: cardosoi is found in eastern Amazonian Brazil south of the Amazon between the Tapajós and Xingu rivers and south into Mato Grosso state

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies of the uniform woodcreeper is found in the Amazon Basin south of the Amazon River in Brazil between the Madeira and Xingu rivers and south to Mato Grosso state and northeastern Bolivia's Santa Cruz Department

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Xingu scythebill is found in eastern Amazonian Brazil south of the Amazon between the Xingu and Tocantins rivers and south into Mato Grosso state

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: ochraceiventris is found in Brazil south of the Amazon from the Tapajós and its tributary Teles Pires rivers east to the Xingu River. The yellow-browed antbird inhabits the understorey to mid-storey of the interior of terra firme forest and mature secondary forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Tapajós hermit is found in Brazil between the Tapajós and Xingu Rivers, north-flowing tributaries of the Amazon River
